title: RFE: Add --ignore/-i support to the build command (Original title: Podman's dockerignore build context support?)
body:
As described in this issue: https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/12886
Does Podman support this same functionality?
Thanks!

comments.body: Thanks! And moreover, I'm investigating how build contexts work when there are multiple Dockerfiles and potentially multiple docker-compose.yml files within a repository - and how one builds them using docker-compose (podman-compose) build.

According the the Moby link above.  You would like us to use Dockerfile.dockerignore and Containerfile.dockerignore

IE if I do a buildah bud -f /PATHTO/foobar /tmp, then we should grab
/PATHTO/foobar.dockerignore if it exists, otherwise we would look for /tmp/.dockerignore.

buildah bud and podman build has `--ignorefile` now.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
